Resolving of disputes by negotiations-On receipt tlerence regardii‘ig a dispute the Central Government :all a meeting of the representatives of the concerned Government(s) and/or the Chairman/ViceChairman, naputra‘ Board and 'shall' endeavodr'td‘re‘solve the re through negotiations. 4. Resolving-of disputes by concifiation.— (1) The all Government may refer the disputes, for conciliation : Chairman, Central Water Commission, who shalltrynciliate thedispute andshall take all such steps, as he deem fit, for purposes of enabling the parties to the te to come to a settle'menL (2) The Conciliator shall complete the task assigned1: within six months from the date for the first meeting1:: Central Governmentmaj grant suitable extermion of upto a period of six months if a request in that behalf dc by either the Conciliator or any of'the parties to the re. (2) The terms and conditions of appointment of tlm = Arbitrator under sub—rule (1),shall be such as may be. determined by the Central Government in consultation with the Chief Justice of India. , ' (3) After the Arbitrator is appointed by tthhiefJustice of India under sub-rule (‘1')'The Central Government shall refer the dispute to'the Arbitrator7'1n a self contained note containing inter alia; the following information :- (a) (b) (C) Theparties to the dispute; Specific matter or matters'm dispute within“ details thereof ; Any other matter connected with or relevant to the dispute; ‘ Efforts, if any, made by the parties to the dispute themselves or by the Central Government through the negotiations and/or conciliation as under rules 3 and 4 to settle the matter(s) in dispute; and ' The'views of the aggrieved party/partiesonthe matter or matters in dispute.
